concerns.text: FR-009 specifies that the sensitivity sweep should start at the sparsest threshold (10%) and, if results conflict, report nodes significant at the sparsest *connected* threshold (10%). FR-013, however, dictates that when the default threshold (15%) yields a disconnected graph the system must *increase* the threshold (20%, 25%, …) and never attempts a lower threshold such as 10%. These two requirements prescribe mutually exclusive strategies for handling a disconnected graph, creating an internal contradiction.
